description_long: This object is a large, stylized flower with five broad, pointed petals radiating symmetrically from a central point. The petals are predominantly deep maroon with lighter greenish hues near the center, and each petal features several distinct oval-shaped holes or cutouts. The surface of the petals displays a subtle veining pattern, adding to the naturalistic appearance. The flower sits very close to the ground, with no visible stem or supporting structure. The overall form is flat and spread out, making it appear as if it is lying flush against a surface. The coloration and exaggerated size suggest it could serve as a decorative or thematic element in an urban park or garden setting.
description: A large, flat flower with five maroon and green veined petals, each featuring oval cutouts and radiating symmetrically from the center.
description_view_0: The flower is seen from a slightly elevated angle, showing the full spread of all five petals and the central green area.
description_view_1: This view highlights the rightmost petal, making its curvature and cutouts more prominent, while the left petals recede.
description_view_2: The leftmost petal is more visible, and the central green area appears slightly more pronounced from this angle.
description_view_3: The lower petals are more foreshortened, and the upper petals dominate the view, emphasizing the symmetry and cutouts.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, with five equally spaced petals radiating symmetrically from the center. There is no visible layering or overlap, and the arrangement is whorled with clear bilateral symmetry.
